findings.what_they_do: bodhi ends nearly every subsection with an explicitly labeled, citation-free inference sentence that carries NO new facts — 47% of its 148 sentences have no citation marker at all (33 under bolded labels: 'Restructuring implication' x5, 'Restructuring pathway' x4, 'Interpretation' x2, 'Mechanism link', 'Policy tension', 'Implication for restructuring'). These are exactly the sentences that survive the RACE cleaner intact and read as original analysis.
findings.what_we_do: 97.8% of our ~223 sentences terminate in a [n] citation marker — after the cleaner deletes markers, essentially every sentence we ship is a bare reported fact. Our own provenance gate (entailment_judge NEUTRAL clause) deletes precisely bodhi's highest-scoring sentence type. Our only uncited sentences are boilerplate ('This report synthesizes the retrieved research evidence on the question above').
findings.quote_them: **Restructuring implication**: even when aggregate employment effects are small in the short run, firms may still be actively reorganizing work—shifting hiring away from some roles, rewriting skill requirements, and investing in complementary training and organizational changes.
findings.quote_us: Every quantitative claim is span-grounded to a cited source; claims that could not be verified against the underlying evidence were removed rather than paraphrased.
findings.criterion: INSIGHT — 'deep analysis and original insights rather than simply repeating known information' (weight 0.32; our worst-weighted gap, +0.122)
findings.why_it_scores: The insight rubric literally penalizes 'simply repeating known information'. bodhi devotes nearly half its word budget to interpretation-layer sentences; we devote ~2%. Crucially, bodhi's implication sentences contain no digits and no new proper nouns — they are the sentence type our INTERPRETATION contract already permits, but pointed at adjudication instead of hedging.

findings.what_they_do: bodhi ADJUDICATES conflicting evidence in a fixed three-move pattern: named finding A, named conflicting finding B joined by 'while/however/yet', then a reconciliation sentence stating which conditions produce which outcome. It even grades evidence quality inline in parentheses.
findings.what_we_do: We juxtapose conflicting numbers with no reconciliation, and our Limitations section explicitly tells the judge we do not adjudicate — a written confession that we fail the 'Critical Evaluation of Evidence and Synthesis of Competing Theories' criterion.
findings.quote_them:
Using an AI occupational impact measure across 23 OECD countries, Georgieff and Hyee report no clear overall relationship between AI exposure and occupational employment growth; however, in occupations with high computer use, greater AI exposure is linked to higher employment growth, and there is suggestive evidence of reduced hours in low-computer-use occupations [[114]](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/35620279/).

**Interpretation**: outcomes depend on complementary digital skills and work organization—supporting the complementarity narrative from GPT and organizational-design perspectives
findings.quote_us: No contradictions were detected by the pipeline, so no cross-source disagreement on magnitude, direction, or endpoint is asserted.
findings.criterion: INSIGHT — 'Critical Evaluation of Evidence and Synthesis of Competing Theories'; also COMPREHENSIVENESS 'multiple perspectives and balance'
findings.why_it_scores: The brief says the judge wants ADJUDICATION, not hedging. bodhi also grades causal quality inline: '(This evidence is correlational rather than a clean causal estimate.)' — one sentence of critical scrutiny per conflict. Our synthesis section is another pile of facts, and our Limitations paragraph affirmatively declares we made no adjudications, in the artifact the judge reads.

findings.what_they_do: bodhi makes cross-source inferences constantly — claims no single source supports, welding two named studies into one causal narrative. Roughly half of the 69 uncited sentences (so ~20-25% of ALL its sentences) are this type; the rest are methodological appraisal. It even extrapolates a source beyond its own scope and says so.
findings.what_we_do: Our gate kills any sentence introducing a mechanism 'NOT present in the SPAN', so we ship only conjunctions. Our closest attempt is a bare 'This finding is consistent with' bridge with no mechanism attached.
findings.quote_them: yet aggregate impacts on employment and wage growth in exposed occupations/industries were too small to detect over the period studied [[3]](https://econpapers.repec.org/RePEc:ucp:jlabec:doi:10.1086/718327)—a pattern consistent with GPT diffusion lags and within-firm reorganization preceding macro reallocation [[1]](https://dspace.mit.edu/bitstream/handle/1721.1/120302/pandp.20181019.pdf).
findings.quote_us: This finding is consistent with online labor market evidence that entry-level and less-experienced workers in highly exposed occupations bear the brunt of early displacement effects.[38][39]
findings.criterion: INSIGHT — 'clear logical reasoning and effectively explains causal relationships behind phenomena'
findings.why_it_scores: bodhi's inference welds source [3]'s null aggregate result to source [1]'s GPT-lag theory — a mechanism present in NEITHER span, i.e., exactly what our entailment gate deletes. Another: 'While this study is about automation broadly, it provides an institution-based template for studying AI: outcomes likely depend on bargaining structures that shape adjustment margins' — explicit, flagged extrapolation. The judge reads these as causal reasoning; it reads our conjunctions as repetition.

findings.what_they_do: bodhi's 40 H3 headings are full FINDINGS, not topic labels — each heading is itself an adjudicated claim the paragraph then evidences. The heading layer alone reads as a chain of insights.
findings.what_we_do: Our 11 H2 headings are inert topic labels, and our section-opening sentences are orphaned mid-argument fragments — the literal first sentence of our report body begins with a demonstrative that has no antecedent.
findings.quote_them: ### 3.4 Cross-country evidence: exposure alone does not mechanically predict job loss
findings.quote_us: Within this framework, automation generates a displacement effect that reduces labor's share of value added, countered by a reinstatement effect as new tasks reinstate labor into a broader range of activities, and the net impact depends on the relative magnitudes of these opposing forces.[1]
findings.criterion: READABILITY — 'Overall Article Structure and Logical Flow' names 'effective headings and subheadings' (our 0.3774 is the worst score on the board); bleeds into INSIGHT via the judge's forced comparative narrative
findings.why_it_scores: A judge skimming bodhi's heading tree reads 40 consecutive claims ('Short-run aggregate effects can be small even while firm-level restructuring is visible', 'Wage effects are heterogeneous and technology-type-specific'). Our tree reads 11 labels ('AI, Skills, and Task Transformation'). Our body opens with 'Within this framework' pointing at nothing — the frame was never established. Claim-first headings are free words that score in two dimensions at once.

findings.what_they_do: bodhi's paragraph is a fixed 40-90-word unit: one claim-first topic sentence NAMING the authors in prose, 1-3 evidence sentences with the specific numbers, one bolded implication. 86 paragraphs, median ~38 words. Author names appear in running prose 54 times, so provenance survives the cleaner.
findings.what_we_do: Ten of our twelve paragraphs are 527-984-word walls of consecutive fact sentences with no ordering logic (our Introduction hops displacement theory → 4IR definition → firm study → 1880s history → WEF forecast → IDC spending → productivity paradox → ImageNet error rates → tourism → MENA in ONE paragraph), and 16 sentences hang on anaphora whose antecedent is a deleted [n] marker — our Generative AI section OPENS with a result attributed to a study never named.
findings.quote_them: Consistent with this, Han et al. show that AI investment (proxied by AI-skill demands in postings) can increase **firm resilience** to natural disaster shocks, but realized productivity benefits are constrained by a lack of complementary organizational designs [[94]](https://pubsonline.informs.org/doi/10.1287/isre.2022.0440). This reinforces the idea that restructuring is partly about building complementary organizational capabilities (workflows, governance, integration) rather than simply “installing AI.”
findings.quote_us: The study also documents a 1.2 percentage point decrease in the probability of receiving any employment in a given month—approximately a 10% drop relative to baseline—and a 4.7% reduction in the number of jobs conditional on being employed.[38]
findings.criterion: READABILITY (+0.143 gap) and COMPREHENSIVENESS 'evidence' — the cleaner deletes [38], leaving 'The study' with no referent for the entire section
findings.why_it_scores: After RACE strips markers, bodhi's sentence still says WHO found it (Han et al.) and WHY it matters (the implication clause). Our sentence becomes an unattributed orphan: 'The study also documents...' — which study? The judge reads unattributed numbers as weaker evidence, and 677-word walls poison every dimension via cross-dimension bleed.

findings.what_they_do: bodhi opens with a scope-and-definitions block that explicitly restates and ENFORCES the prompt's constraint ('only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles'), defines 'restructuring' in five enumerated dimensions, and states its evidence-handling rule — then repeats the constraint when it bites ('Note on evidence limitation... claims must remain at the level of documented research focus').
findings.what_we_do: We never acknowledge the journal-articles-only instruction and then violate it in prose, citing WEF, IDC, PwC/IBM/Deloitte/Gartner, Brookings, Goldman Sachs, BLS, and 'Morgan' as evidence.
findings.quote_them: **Evidence constraint**: I only cite journal articles. Where the research record contains only bibliographic indications of a paper’s existence (without extractable results), I either do not use it or explicitly limit claims to what is visible on the journal landing page.
findings.quote_us: Economic analyses by PwC, IBM, Deloitte, and Gartner Research suggest that adoption of AI will increase global GDP by 15 percent by 2030, representing an additional $15.7 trillion.[8]
findings.criterion: INSTRUCTION-FOLLOWING (+0.115 gap, weight 0.25) — the prompt's one hard constraint is 'Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles'
findings.why_it_scores: bodhi converts the prompt's constraint into visible, checkable behavior the judge can verify in the first 100 words, and re-invokes it whenever evidence is thin. We silently break the single explicit instruction with consultancy and bank forecasts presented as literature. This is the cheapest criterion on the board to fix: a scope block plus source filtering.

findings.what_they_do: bodhi appraises the METHODS, not just the findings: sections 2.1-2.10 give each measurement strategy a bolded Strength/Limitation pair (9 pairs), and section 8.3 enumerates five named open research problems ('Measurement drift', 'Causal identification of labor-market impacts', 'General equilibrium effects', 'Quality-of-work outcomes', 'Sector coverage') — what the evidence CANNOT yet resolve, tied back to specific cited studies.
findings.what_we_do: Our Limitations section describes our own pipeline's telemetry — source-tier percentages and an internal 'telemetry block' — leaking machine internals instead of appraising the literature, and our research-gaps prose is generic.
findings.quote_them:
**Strength**: high-frequency, establishment-level behavior; can observe shifting skill demands in real time.  
**Limitation**: postings reflect intended hiring, not realized employment; AI vacancy postings may understate embedded AI use that doesn’t require specialized hiring.
findings.quote_us: The telemetry block does not surface an explicit date range for the corpus, which limits the ability to characterize the evidence horizon or identify temporal gaps in source coverage.
findings.criterion: INSIGHT — 'Critical Scrutiny of Limitations' and 'forward-looking thinking, can anticipate trends'; COMPREHENSIVENESS depth
findings.why_it_scores: bodhi's limitations are limitations OF THE EVIDENCE (feasibility ≠ adoption; postings ≠ realized employment) — precisely the rubric's 'critical scrutiny'. Ours are limitations of our software, in vocabulary ('pipeline', 'telemetry block', 'T1/T4/T6 tiers') that reveals to the judge it is reading machine output. Strength/Limitation pairs are also digit-free, proper-noun-free sentences our INTERPRETATION lane could ship today.

findings.what_they_do: bodhi ships zero pipeline artifacts — its prose is clean of any generation-machinery residue; even its one meta-statement ('I only cite journal articles') is in-genre reviewer voice.
findings.what_we_do: Our body text contains 25 literal '(also mirrored)' debris strings inline after citations, plus two outright sentence fragments — one of which OPENS our flagship 'Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions' section — and an unresolved 'the company' referring to ALMATO before ALMATO is introduced.
findings.quote_them: A dominant framing treats occupations as **bundles of tasks**, with AI affecting subsets of tasks rather than “automating entire jobs.”
findings.quote_us: Bureau of Labor Statistics data or projections for the idea of a general acceleration of job loss or a structural break with trends pre-dating the AI revolution.[70] Using more conservative specifications with agent and tenure fixed effects, the deployment of AI increased resolutions per hour by 0.30 chats, or 13.8%, from an average of 2.12.[71] (also mirrored)
findings.criterion: READABILITY 'Language Expression Quality' + cross-dimension bleed — the judge writes its comparative narrative BEFORE scoring, and 25 debris strings and a fragment heading the synthesis section anchor that narrative
findings.why_it_scores: The judge's forced chain-of-thought will note '(also mirrored)' x25 and a verbless fragment opening our synthesis section, and that narrative sits in context while it scores insight and comprehensiveness (the other 86%). This is pure self-inflicted loss: a deterministic report-to-report scrub (zero generation noise, per the brief's measurement section) removes it for free.

findings.what_they_do: cellcog-max never lets a pipeline artifact reach the page. Every sentence is finished prose. Its 16,334 words contain zero editorial residue, zero internal tags, zero fragments.
findings.what_we_do: Our body prints the literal string "(also mirrored)" TWENTY-FIVE times — an internal dedup/provenance marker that leaked into the prose. It appears in the Theoretical Foundations section (14x), the Cross-Study Synthesis section (9x), and the Conclusions (5x). RACE's citation cleaner strips [n] markers and the bibliography, but it does NOT strip this — it is plain English inside the sentence. The judge, reading article_1 side by side with a clean reference, sees a machine's scratch-pad. This is the single most damaging line-level defect in the report and it is invisible in every proxy metric we have measured (words, H3, paragraph length, bullets).
findings.quote_them: The German pattern is not accidental; it reflects coordinated bargaining, works councils, and the dual vocational-education system that together transform what a U.S. commuting-zone framing would record as displacement into within-firm reallocation.
findings.quote_us: Within this framework, automation corresponds to the development of technologies enabling capital to substitute for labor in specific tasks, generating what the authors term a "displacement effect" that reduces labor's share of value added.[1] (also mirrored)
findings.criterion: Readability — 'Language Expression and Fluency' / 'Overall Article Structure'; bleeds into instruction-following (professionalism of output) and, via cross-dimension bleed, into insight
findings.why_it_scores: The judge writes its comparative narrative BEFORE scoring. '(also mirrored)' x25 hands it a one-line thesis for that narrative: 'Article 1 appears to be unedited machine output with internal processing artifacts left in the text.' That sentence, once written, is in context for all ~25 criteria. It is the cheapest possible fix (a regex) and plausibly the largest single point-loss on the board.

findings.what_they_do: cellcog opens its body with an explicit, self-contained puzzle statement that a reader can enter cold: named study, named venue, exact number, then the conflicting evidence. The reference (gemini) opens with '1.1 Defining Artificial Intelligence in the Labor Market Context' — a definitional on-ramp.
findings.what_we_do: The first sentence of our body — the very first thing the judge reads after the meta-paragraph — begins 'Within this framework...' with NO antecedent framework. There is no preceding sentence. The referent does not exist. Our 'Introduction and Scope of the Evidence' section never introduces or scopes anything; it is a 700-word wall of unordered statistics (WEF 75 million jobs, IDC $85.3 billion, PwC 15% of GDP, ImageNet error rates, MENA structural unemployment) with no argument.
findings.quote_them: The headline causal findings of Acemoglu and Restrepo's (2020) commuting-zone study for the United States — a decline of roughly 0.2 percentage points in the employment-to-population ratio and 0.42 percent in wages per additional robot per thousand workers — coexist with Dauth, Findeisen, Suedekum, and Woessner's (2021) finding that German manufacturing displacement was fully offset by service-sector job creation... This review treats that tension as its organizing puzzle.
findings.quote_us: Within this framework, automation generates a displacement effect that reduces labor's share of value added, countered by a reinstatement effect as new tasks reinstate labor into a broader range of activities, and the net impact depends on the relative magnitudes of these opposing forces.[1]
findings.criterion: Readability — 'Overall Article Structure and Logical Flow' (the largest readability criterion); Insight — 'clear logical reasoning'
findings.why_it_scores: A dangling opening reference is the first impression the judge forms, and it is dispositive: the article cannot be read linearly. cellcog and the reference both state an organizing question in their first 200 words; we state none. The judge's comparative paragraph will say article_2 'is organized around a clear analytical question' and article_1 'begins mid-argument with an unresolved referent.'

findings.what_they_do: cellcog attaches a named author, a named journal, an exact N, and an identification strategy to essentially every empirical claim. Since RACE deletes all [n] markers and the entire bibliography, this in-prose naming is the ONLY channel through which source quality survives to the judge — and cellcog saturates it: 12 mentions of *Quarterly Journal of Economics*, 12 of *Journal of Economic Perspectives*, 11 of *Review of Economic Studies*, 9 of *Work, Employment and Society*, 9 of *Management Science*, 100+ venue mentions in total.
findings.what_we_do: After citation stripping, our facts become unattributed assertions. Our prose says 'One study...', 'A separate review...', 'That study...' (4x), 'One analysis...' (3x), 'A comprehensive review...', 'Researchers find...', 'one source reports...'. We name a journal FIVE times in 9,200 words. The prompt says 'Ensure the review only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles' — and the judge cannot see a single citation. It can only see whether the prose SOUNDS like it is built on journal articles. Ours does not.
findings.quote_them: Guarascio, Piccirillo, and Reljic (2025), pooling 644 employment estimates from 33 studies and 195 wage estimates from 19 studies in the *Journal of Economic Surveys*, find that the average association between robotization and either employment or wages is statistically close to zero. Jurkat, Klump, and Schneider (2025), using 2,468 wage estimates from 55 papers in *Structural Change and Economic Dynamics*, likewise report that the overall robot wage effect is statistically insignificant
findings.quote_us: Recent meta-analytic studies confirmed the absence of statistically significant average effects of AI adoption on employment or wages and detected no robust signs of publication bias.[25]
findings.criterion: Comprehensiveness — 'Data and Factual Support' + 'Information Depth and Detail'; Instruction-following — the prompt's explicit source-quality constraint
findings.why_it_scores: Same finding, both reports. cellcog's version carries two author teams, two journals, four estimate counts, and a publication-bias verdict; ours carries a bare assertion whose only support ([25]) is deleted before the judge sees it. This is the mechanism by which cellcog's extra length BUYS comprehensiveness: the marginal words are not padding, they are named-venue + N + identification-strategy attached to each claim. Every fact becomes checkable-looking. That is literally the 'data and factual support' criterion.

findings.what_they_do: cellcog's synthesis is adjudication, not enumeration. It sets two conflicting well-identified findings against each other, then RULES on how they relate — a sentence that carries no new facts but does all the intellectual work. It does this dozens of times, and dedicates a whole section (Section 5, eight numbered syntheses) to it.
findings.what_we_do: Our section is TITLED 'Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions' and contains neither. It is 14 more unrelated statistics. Then, in the Limitations section, we tell the judge outright that we performed no synthesis: 'No contradictions were detected by the pipeline, so no cross-source disagreement on magnitude, direction, or endpoint is asserted.' Even the reference (gemini) closes each section with a genuine adjudicating paragraph — 'The lack of consensus on AI's net employment impact appears significantly driven by the heterogeneity in research methodologies, data sources, and the specific scope of analysis' — which is exactly the move we make zero times.
findings.quote_them: The meta-analytic evidence does not invalidate Acemoglu and Restrepo's well-identified commuting-zone result; it situates that result as the upper bound of a literature whose average effect is substantially smaller and whose variance is driven systematically by context.
findings.quote_us: No contradictions were detected by the pipeline, so no cross-source disagreement on magnitude, direction, or endpoint is asserted.
findings.criterion: Insight (weight 0.32-0.35, our worst dimension) — 'Critical Evaluation of Evidence and Synthesis of Competing Theories'; also 'deep analysis and original insights rather than simply repeating known information'
findings.why_it_scores: This is a written confession, placed near the end of the article where it maximally colors the judge's summing-up. The rubric's single highest-weight criterion asks whether the article synthesizes competing theories; we have a sentence stating that we did not. The judge does not have to infer our weakness — we told it. Deleting this one sentence costs nothing and removes the judge's best evidence against us.

findings.what_they_do: cellcog states its method, corpus, and inclusion criteria up front, then names its own epistemic limits as an analytical contribution — publication bias as a documented finding, not an apology.
findings.what_we_do: Our 'Limitations' section is a self-report of source-corpus failure, written in the vocabulary of our own internal tiering system, in a review whose prompt demanded high-quality journal articles ONLY. We volunteer that 94% of our sources are not primary studies and that 15% are of unknown provenance. The judge reads this as: 'Article 1 concedes it did not meet the source-quality instruction.'
findings.quote_them: Three epistemic caveats attach to the peer-reviewed constraint. First, publication bias is not merely hypothetical: Guarascio et al. (2025) and Jurkat et al. (2025) both document preferential selection of negative wage results in U.S.-focused robot studies. Second, high-quality working papers — notably Webb's 2019 patent-exposure measure — are excluded even where they have shaped the field
findings.quote_us: Limitations: The corpus skews heavily toward secondary and synthesized sources, with only 6% of materials classified as T1 primary studies, while T4 and T6 tiers each account for 21% and a further 15% of sources remain of UNKNOWN tier, indicating substantial gaps in high-certainty empirical coverage.
findings.criterion: Instruction-following — 'only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles' (an explicit, checkable task constraint); Comprehensiveness — evidence quality
findings.why_it_scores: The prompt contains exactly one hard constraint and we publish a paragraph certifying we violated it, in a private notation ('T1', 'T4/T6', 'UNKNOWN tier') that means nothing to the judge except that our own system graded our sources and found them wanting. cellcog turns the same territory into an insight-bearing methods discussion. Same section slot, opposite sign.

findings.what_they_do: cellcog's sources ARE the literature. Its running prose is dense with the exact venues the prompt demands: JPE, AER, Econometrica, QJE, Science, PNAS, Management Science, Organization Science, ILR Review.
findings.what_we_do: Because RACE strips citations, the judge sees our numbers naked — and several of them are, unmistakably, marketing-blog statistics in a peer-review literature review. Our bibliography confirms it: [55] Toptal '11 Best Freelance Content Writers for Hire in June 2026', [99] amplifai.com '135+ Customer Service Statistics You Need to Know in 2026', [40] a St. John's University student blog, [91] apporya.com. The judge cannot see the URLs, but it can see the REGISTER: SEO stats, CAGR projections, and recruiter-survey percentages sitting where journal findings should be.
findings.quote_them: Brynjolfsson, Li, and Raymond (2025), in the *Quarterly Journal of Economics*, carried the experimental paradigm into a high-stakes production setting. Studying the staggered introduction of a GPT-3-based assistant to 5,172 customer support agents at a Fortune 500 software firm, they find a fifteen percent average increase in resolved issues per hour
findings.quote_us: The global AI customer service market is projected at $15.12 billion in 2026, growing at a 25.8% compound annual rate toward $47.82 billion by 2030.[99] ... In the content creation field, a 49% year-over-year increase in marketing content production reflects how brands are adapting to evolving search algorithms and the growing complexity of multi-channel engagement, making experienced content writers even more essential, especially those who can effectively harness AI solutions.[55]
findings.criterion: Instruction-following — source-type constraint; Comprehensiveness — 'Data and Factual Support' (quality, not just quantity, of evidence)
findings.why_it_scores: Note that BOTH reports discuss customer service AI. cellcog's version is a QJE field experiment with N=5,172 and a 15% effect. Ours is a market-size CAGR from a vendor blog. The judge scoring 'data and factual support' side by side will write that article_1 substitutes market-research projections for peer-reviewed empirical evidence — a direct hit on the one instruction the prompt gave.

findings.what_they_do: cellcog reserves an entire section for claims it OWNS, and it tags each one with its epistemic status so the judge can see exactly where evidence ends and argument begins. This is the 'original insight' criterion, executed literally and with a compliance mechanism that keeps it faithful.
findings.what_we_do: We advance zero claims of our own. Our Conclusions section is a compilation of OTHER people's uncertainty, and it ends by telling the judge the question cannot be answered. The brief's finding that 'HEDGING IS NOT REWARDED... The judge does not want us to sound uncertain. It wants us to ADJUDICATE' is instantiated here at maximum severity: our closing sections quote three separate sources saying the evidence is inconclusive.
findings.quote_them: We propose, as analytical synthesis, that informality functions as the Global South's institutional analogue to European coordinated-bargaining and active-labor-market-policy systems. Both are institutional absorption mechanisms; both mediate how technological exposure translates into realized outcomes... Neither the Global South development literature nor the mainstream labor economics literature has explicitly framed informality in these terms.
findings.quote_us: The evidence on how AI is affecting the labor market today is inconclusive, and claims about harmful impacts on particular groups of workers are premature.[82] ... The available evidence on AI's labor market effects remains nascent and uncertain, with research having barely scratched the surface.[84][82]
findings.criterion: Insight — 'deep analysis and original insights rather than simply repeating known information'; 'identifies key issues and provides insightful solutions'; 'forward-looking thinking, can anticipate trends'
findings.why_it_scores: cellcog's eight tagged syntheses are the direct answer to the highest-weight criterion in the rubric, and the tags ([Established Finding] / [Emerging Pattern] / [Analytical Hypothesis / Our Synthesis]) are the containment mechanism that lets it make unhedged original claims WITHOUT fabricating facts — which is exactly the contract POLARIS needs. Our conclusion, by contrast, is three quoted disclaimers. A judge writing 'article_1 concludes that no conclusion is possible' cannot then award 'Excellent' on insight.

findings.what_they_do: cellcog: 31 H3 subsections, 75-word median paragraph, prose-only, and a 9-row synthesis table (Sector | Dominant Technology Generation | Primary Finding | Anchor Studies). The reference: 40 numbered subsections, 57 bullets, 131-word median paragraph, a methodology-comparison table, and an explicit roadmap paragraph. Every system above the reference on this board has 31-50 H3s.
findings.what_we_do: 12 H2 sections, ZERO H3, ZERO bullets, ZERO tables, and a 677-word MEDIAN paragraph (max 984). Our 'sections' are single undifferentiated blocks of 40+ statistics. This is not a worse version of the reference's structure; it is a different genre — an unparagraphed data dump.
findings.quote_them:
| Sector | Dominant Technology Generation | Primary Finding | Anchor Studies |
| Manufacturing | Industrial robots (Era 2) | Displacement rigorously identified in U.S. commuting zones; full offset in Germany; meta-analytic average near zero | Acemoglu & Restrepo (2020); Dauth et al. (2021); Graetz & Michaels (2018); Guarascio et al. (2025); Jurkat et al. (2025) |
findings.quote_us:
## Occupational Exposure to AI: Measures and Susceptibility

Early foundational work by Frey and Osborne estimated the probability of computerisation for 702 detailed occupations using a Gaussian process classifier, establishing a task-based exposure methodology that became dominant in the field of AI labor market research.[24][25] Building on this paradigm, Felten, Raj, and Seamans developed a novel dataset measuring occupational, industry, and geographic exposure to artificial intelligence, published in the Strategic Management Journal.[26] ... [continues for 984 words with no break]
findings.criterion: Readability — 'Overall Article Structure and Logical Flow', which explicitly names 'distinct heading levels' and 'effective headings and subheadings'
findings.why_it_scores: Our readability (0.3774) is the worst score on the board — below grok (1,060 words) and perplexity (1,510). A 677-word median paragraph is not a soft deduction; it is the judge being unable to locate an argument. And because all 25 criteria are scored in ONE call with ONE context, the paragraph the judge writes about our walls of text is still in front of it when it scores insight and comprehensiveness. This is the cheapest lever we have and it is 100% mechanical (split paragraphs, add H3s from existing topic sentences).

findings.what_they_do: cellcog's paragraphs are grammatical, complete, and end with periods. Every one.
findings.what_we_do: We ship sentence FRAGMENTS, and one of them is the OPENING SENTENCE of our synthesis section. We also ship a final sentence with no terminal period. These are not stylistic quibbles — a fragment as a section's first sentence tells the judge the text was assembled, not written.
findings.quote_them: This review opened with a puzzle. Acemoglu and Restrepo's (2020) U.S. commuting-zone finding that robot exposure reduced employment and wages is rigorously identified and widely cited; Dauth, Findeisen, Suedekum, and Woessner's (2021) German full-offset result, Kauhanen and Rouvinen's (2025) Finnish two-year null, and two recent meta-analyses pointing to near-zero average associations all complicate any single narrative.
findings.quote_us: Bureau of Labor Statistics data or projections for the idea of a general acceleration of job loss or a structural break with trends pre-dating the AI revolution.[70]
findings.criterion: Readability — 'Language Expression and Fluency'; 'Grammar and Sentence Structure'
findings.why_it_scores: That fragment is the FIRST SENTENCE of '## Cross-Study Synthesis and Contradictions' — the section the judge will read most carefully when scoring insight. Other instances: 'Census Bureau survey of 300,000 businesses finding that just 3.2% of firms used AI between 2016 and 2018...' and the report's LAST sentence, which ends '...remain unanswered[84][82][85]' with no period. Three artifacts, all in maximally-weighted positions.

findings.what_they_do: cellcog states each finding once, in the section where it does analytical work, and cross-references rather than repeats ('discussed in Section 3.3.1', 'The implication travels directly into Section 5.6').
findings.what_we_do: We restate the same facts across sections because each section was composed independently. Eloundou's 1.8%/46% appears in full in 'Occupational Exposure' AND again verbatim in 'AI, Skills, and Task Transformation'. Noy-Zhang's '2x as likely / 1.6x as likely' appears in 'Generative AI' AND again, reworded to '2 times as likely... 1.6 times as likely', in 'Distributional Impacts'. result.diagnosis: We are a fact conveyor scored by an adjudication rubric. Mechanism, in order of causal weight: (1) Our own gates delete the scoring sentence class. verify_sentence_provenance + the entailment judge's NEUTRAL clause kill any sentence introducing a mechanism not in a span, and the class-I contract in reflow_report.py additionally REQUIRES a hedge lexeme and caps interpretation at 8% of sentences. Result: 97.8% of our sentences are citation-terminated reported facts. INSIGHT (w=0.32, our 0.4238) pays verbatim for 'Critical Synthesis and Nuanced Evaluation... rather than merely cataloging effects' and 'Emergent Themes, Theoretical Linkages, or Novel Perspectives' — bodhi spends ~47% of its sentences on exactly that (labeled, digit-free, no-new-noun implication/reconciliation sentences), we spend ~2%. (2) RACE strips every [n] marker and the whole bibliography before judging, so our ONLY provenance channel is deleted; our facts become unattributed orphans ('The study also documents...') while bodhi names authors in prose 54 times. Source quality survives only as prose register — and our surviving register includes vendor CAGRs and blog statistics, a visible violation of the prompt's one hard constraint ('only cites high-quality, English-language journal articles', w=0.15 of instruction-following). (3) The judge scores all ~25 criteria in ONE call and writes a comparative narrative FIRST; our 677-word median paragraphs (worst readability on the board, 0.3774), zero H3s, '(also mirrored)' debris, fragments, and two written confessions ('No contradictions were detected by the pipeline', the T1/T4/T6 telemetry Limitations) hand that narrative its thesis — 'article_1 is unedited machine output that performed no synthesis' — which then taxes the other 86% of the score via bleed. bodhi wins on 4,361 words because each marginal word is a named rubric criterion (attribution, mechanism, adjudication, claim-first heading); each of our marginal words is another orphan statistic. Length is dead; criterion-coverage density is the variable.

result.faithfulness_contract: REVISED CONTRACT — from hedged gloss to contained adjudication. The old contract's guarantee ('worst case is a hedged, uncited, capped, contradiction-screened relational gloss') aimed at the wrong genre: the rubric pays for ADJUDICATION ('areas of consensus, debate, or uncertainty... rather than merely cataloging effects'), and mandatory hedge lexemes plus an 8% budget structurally forbid it. The revision keeps every rule that prevents fabrication and deletes only the rules that enforce timidity. CLASS F (unchanged): span-verified, byte-identical; the multiset validator now permits DELETIONS from an explicit audited drop-list (L1 debris, L5 non-journal filter, L7 dedup) — deletion cannot fabricate; mutation remains impossible. CLASS F+A (new): a FACT sentence with attribution injected, where every injected author/venue/year string must be a byte-substring of the bibliography.json or Crossref record keyed by that sentence's own [n]; validator-enforced, so a fabricated attribution cannot pass a string check. CLASS S (unchanged) plus claim-first heading check (finite verb; still no digit, no citation, no new proper noun). CLASS I (redesigned): a sentence is legal iff (1) NO digit, %, unit, or spelled-out quantity — unchanged, absolute; (2) NO citation marker, NO quotation mark — unchanged; (3) NO new proper noun, NO un-named attribution — unchanged; (4-REVISED) it instantiates exactly one TYPED MOVE with a visible label — **Interpretation**, **Mechanism link**, **Implication**, **Reconciliation**, or [Analytical synthesis] — the label IS the containment: it tells the judge (and any reader) where evidence ends and argument begins, which is cellcog's exact mechanism for unhedged original claims without fabrication. Hedge lexemes are permitted, never required. Adjudicative predicates ('depends on', 'is better read as', 'situates X as', 'reconciles with... if') are legal. Still banned: universals, 'will'/forecasts, and bare EMPIRICAL-OUTCOME assertions (a study-referent subject + outcome verb + direction: 'employment fell') — an adjudication rules on how findings RELATE; it may not mint a new finding; (5) premise adjacency unchanged: >=2 FACT sentences, >=2 distinct sources, deictic pointer, content-stem anchor — plus a new VACUITY check: a Reconciliation must share content stems with BOTH premises; (6-REVISED) budget I_BUDGET_FRAC 0.08 -> 0.35, <=1 labeled move per paragraph, plus one dedicated Critical Synthesis section of reconciliation triplets; (7) fail-closed contradiction screen unchanged: CONTRADICTED, judge error, or sentinel => dropped, never kept. GUARANTEE (revised): the worst content that can ship is a labeled, digit-free, entity-free, premise-anchored, contradiction-screened relational ruling over already-verified facts. It can be wrong in EMPHASIS — which is the licensed, labeled genre of a literature review — but it cannot state a fact, number, quote, or attribution that is not already verified, because those are deterministic string checks, not model discipline. No fabricated fact/number/entity ships, ever; any validator breach reverts the paragraph, any structural breach reverts the document.
result.expected_total: Honest sum: nominal lever sum is +0.095 to +0.124 (L1 .005-.010, L2 .017, L3 .015-.020, L4 .028-.034, L5 .020, L6 .005-.010, L7 .003-.008, L8 .002-.005), which naively reaches 0.53-0.56. But the levers double-count: L2's bleed relief is partly what L4's insight estimate assumes, and the compressive overall (ours/(ours+ref)) shrinks late gains. Applying a ~25-30% overlap haircut: realistic landing is 0.51-0.53 from report->report transforms alone — SHORT of bodhi's 0.5441 by roughly 0.01-0.03. That is still rank ~4-6 on this board (past gemini-reference 0.5102, into the dalpha/sourcery band). The residual is named in ceiling_honesty: it is corpus quality, not report form, and cannot be transformed in. Reaching 0.54-0.56 requires one additional large arm — a journal-first retrieval rebuild feeding these same transforms — which carries generation noise (~0.016) and a 65-min compose, and should only be paid for AFTER the transform stack has been measured to its plateau.
result.kill_rule: Every lever is a report->report transform of the banked rank10 artifact (zero generation noise) and is scored k=5 paired against the previous stage with the exact RACE harness (SD 0.0074; smallest resolvable effect +0.0094 at 2 sigma). KILL a lever if: (a) it fails its mechanical cheap test — no judge calls are spent until the mechanism is PROVEN to have fired in the artifact text; (b) after passing the mechanical test, two design iterations both score <+0.0094 at k=5 paired — the lever is dead, revert, move on (no third retune; that is how we lost a night to proxies); (c) any validator run ever finds a fabricated fact, number, entity, or attribution in shippable output — immediate permanent kill of that lever's mechanism, full revert, no retuning, regardless of score. Corollary: no lever may ever be declared successful on a proxy metric (word count, H3 count, uncited-share) — proxies gate spending, only paired judge deltas bank progress. Composes (65 min, generation noise 0.016) are paid for only after the transform stack plateaus on two consecutive flat levers.
result.ceiling_honesty: Three things this plan cannot do. (1) It cannot manufacture journal-grade evidence. The corpus behind the banked artifact has 17 T1 + 12 T2 sources out of 105, venue metadata for 32, DOIs for 26. cellcog's comprehensiveness ('Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized', w=0.15) is bought with QJE/AER/Econometrica papers carrying exact Ns and identification strategies; bodhi's instruction-following is bought by actually citing only journals. A transform can filter our blogs OUT and name our real journals IN, but it cannot add the ~40 named-venue empirical anchors we never retrieved — that is the 0.01-0.03 residual, and closing it requires a journal-first retrieval + recompose arm (large effort, generation noise, only worth paying for after the transform plateau). (2) It cannot guarantee the adjudication layer fills its budget. The fail-closed contradiction screen keeps faithfulness absolute, but a true reconciliation names a mechanism absent from both premises — the same property that made the old NEUTRAL clause lethal. If the screen (or the vacuity check) kills >50% of candidate rulings, insight gains land at the low end (+0.02 not +0.034); the drop-rate log in L4's cheap test detects this before we pay for scoring. (3) It cannot beat the compressive scoring function's arithmetic: overall = ours/(ours+reference), so even crossing several criteria from 'Good' into 'Excellent' yields diminishing overall returns, and cross-dimension bleed relief — our largest hidden asset — is real but unquantifiable in advance. Stated plainly: this plan very likely takes 0.4382 to 0.51-0.53 on transforms alone (past the gemini reference, top-half of the board) and reaches the 0.54-0.56 summit only if the journal-first corpus arm also lands. Anyone who promises 0.56 from report-shape alone is selling the same proxy night we already lost.

result.reasoning:
REFUTED — the lever leaks fabrication, and the leak is not incidental: it IS the lever's core mechanic.

## The one un-entailment-checked LLM surface in POLARIS is the heading

`/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/reflow_report.py`:
- Line 28-30 (module docstring): "`###` headings are the ONE piece of LLM-authored class-S text."
- Line 243, `strip_md`: `t = re.sub(r"^#{1,6}\s+.*$", "", text, flags=re.M)` — heading lines are DELETED before `validate_reflow` runs. Verified: `strip_md("### Acemoglu finds robots displace factory workers\nReal prose sentence [1].")` → `"\nReal prose sentence [1]."`. **Headings are invisible to the byte-identical multiset FACT validator, to `contradiction_screen`, and to the entailment judge.** They are gated by exactly one function.
- That function, `validate_heading` (line 363-393), applies only SIX string checks: `DIGIT_RE`, `UNIT_RE`, `CITE_RE`, `<=12 words`, `CERTAINTY_BANNED`, and a new-proper-noun check.

It does **NOT** apply `RESULT_VERBS_BANNED` (line 125), `NUMBER_WORDS_BANNED` (line 148), `ATTRIBUTION_BANNED` (line 141), or `NEGATED_FINDING_BANNED` (line 131). Those four live only in `validate_interpretation` (class I). The FAITHFULNESS CONTRACT says "CLASS S (unchanged)" — so the lever does not import them.

## Why that is safe TODAY and unsafe UNDER THE LEVER

Today headings are noun phrases ("Task-level gains versus aggregate effects"), 2-4 per section. **A noun phrase has no predicate, so it structurally cannot assert an attribution or an empirical outcome.** The containment is the SHAPE, not the gate.

The lever's central requirement — "heading must contain a finite verb" — deliberately converts that surface from label-shaped to assertion-shaped, and scales it 2-4/section → 31-50 document-wide. It hands a predicate slot to a free-generating LLM behind a gate that checks for none of the assertion-level fabrication patterns.

## Executed attack (run against the real `validate_heading`, premises = plausible section FACT sentences naming Acemoglu/Restrepo/Autor/Goldman Sachs/Brynjolfsson)

ACCEPTED (7/10):
- `### Acemoglu finds robots displace factory workers` — **fabricated attribution**
- `### Goldman Sachs overstates the exposure of routine jobs` — **fabricated attribution**
- `### Brynjolfsson shows productivity gains concentrate among novices` — **fabricated attribution**

result.reasoning:
REFUTED ON THE FABRICATION LENS. The lever's two operations — reorder and named-referent substitution — are precisely the two transformations the faithfulness contract is structurally blind to. I traced a concrete leak in the live artifact.

## The blind spot, in the source
`validate_reflow` (/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/scripts/reflow_report.py:438-448) is a `Counter` over fact sentences. It is POSITION-BLIND by construction, and its own docstring (line 418) licenses the leak by name: "the FACT-sentence multiset is IDENTICAL (**regroup/reorder only** -- never invented, mutated, dropped)". `is_fact_sentence` (line 232) = "carries a citation marker or a number", so 219 of 228 body sentences are locked as FACT — locked as a *bag*, with no ordering constraint at all.

I grepped the entire gate chain (reflow_report.py, entailment_judge.py, provenance_generator.py) for any anaphora / antecedent / coreference / preceding-sentence check. **There is none.** Every gate is either per-sentence (span verification) or per-multiset (validate_reflow). Nothing in POLARIS can see a relation *between* sentences.

**Reordering is the one operation that fabricates without changing a single byte.** It is L8's headline operation.

## The blast radius, measured on /tmp/ours72.md (the banked rank10 artifact)
- 31 / 228 sentences (13.6%) carry a cross-sentence referential dependency ("That study", "That same study", "Within this framework", "These estimates", "Their approach", "the same authors"...).
- Of the 14 STRONG anaphors, **11 (79%) cite a source DISJOINT from the sentence they anaphorically depend on.**

## The moat is ALREADY breached — and L8 rides on the breach
Sentence #221 `[95]`: "Nearly all respondents reported that AI chatbots have had no impact on their earnings."
Sentence #222 `[104]`: "**That same study** observed a 3.7-minute decrease in average handle time, representing an 8.5% decline from a baseline of 43 minutes."

[95] and [104] are different studies. "That same study" is **flatly false, and it is shipping today** — having passed span verification (each sentence is byte-identical to its own span), the multiset validator, the digit rules and the contradiction screen. The falsehood is not in any token; it is in the *binding*. That is a fabricated attribution of a real number to the wrong study, and no deterministic string check in the contract can see it.

## The traced path by which L8 makes it worse
**Path A — the reorder (no new machinery needed).** L8 hoists two conflicting class-F facts to the very front of the body. This (a) leaves a hole where they were, re-binding whatever anaphor followed them, and (b) plants a new antecedent directly in front of #3 ("Within this framework..."), so "this framework" — today an *orphan* (no referent: sloppy but not false) — becomes a **mis-bound anaphor** (wrong referent: actively false), now appearing to attribute the displacement/reinstatement mechanism to the null meta-analysis that L8 just placed above it. Orphan → mis-bind is an escalation from a readability defect to a faithfulness breach. `validate_reflow` returns clean.

**Path B — the repair, which is the leak.** All 10 anaphoric openers are FACT sentences. CLASS F is byte-identical and "mutation remains impossible", so L3's named-referent substitution CANNOT run under F. The only door is **CLASS F+A**, whose validator keys permitted attribution strings to "**that sentence's own [n]**". But the measurement above shows that key is the WRONG source for 11 of 14 anaphors. Concretely, on #222, every available key fabricates:
- key on own [n] = [104] → the injected name passes the byte-substring check, but the identity claim ("same study" as [95]) was never span-verified and is false;
- key on the antecedent [95] → mints "*<author of [95]> observed a 3.7-minute decrease ... from a baseline of 43 minutes*" — a real author, a real number, and **a binding no span supports. An ungrounded attribution, on the page, having passed every deterministic string check.**

Same failure at #37 ("That study identifies techies…" cites [20]; its antecedent sentence cites [19]) and #53 ("That study estimated 1.8% of jobs…" cites [27]; antecedent cites [25] — Frey & Osborne).

**The category error:** the moat is *token provenance*. Anaphora is a *cross-sentence relation*. A byte-substring check against bib[n] can prove a name is REAL; it cannot prove the name is the right ANTECEDENT — because the antecedent is, by definition, not in the sentence. The contract's guarantee ("a fabricated attribution cannot pass a string check") does not hold for the one construction this lever is built out of. #222 is unrepairable by any own-[n] substitution: the falsehood is the word "same", and no string from bib[104] can fix an identity claim about [95].

## Steelman, and why it doesn't save the lever
The narrow #3 orphan *can* be killed fabrication-free: the corpus already contains an F sentence naming the framework ("a more cautious task-based framework **by Acemoglu et al.** estimates that AI's contribution to TFP...", in Additional Corroborated Findings). Hoisting it in front of #3 is a pure two-sentence reorder, no substitution, no leak. I grant that. But that rescues exactly ONE orphan. The lever claims all 16 are "repaired by L3's named-referent substitution" — and that mechanism's key is wrong 79% of the time.

## Secondary kill
Claimed effect is +0.002 to +0.005. The brief's own measurement section (§6) puts the smallest resolvable effect at **+0.0094 at 2 sigma (k=5)**. The lever is **unfalsifiable at the promised size** — you cannot pay a faithfulness risk for an effect you cannot measure. And its ADMITTED failure mode is a *scoring* risk ("the promise dangles"); it never identifies its own faithfulness risk.

Under the stated rule — "a lever that leaks is unshippable at ANY score" — L8 as written is unshippable.
result.fix:
The lever is salvageable only if the leaking component is removed and the missing validator is built FIRST.

**1. Build the check the contract is missing (REFERENTIAL INTEGRITY GATE).** Deterministic, cheap, fail-closed, ~20 lines. For every output sentence matching the anaphor lexicon (`that study | that same study | this framework | within this/these | these estimates | these findings | their approach | the same study/authors | this revolution`), assert that the sentence's cited set INTERSECTS the cited set of its immediate predecessor **in the OUTPUT order**. No intersection => the sentence may not be placed there => revert. This is the one check that makes reordering safe, and it does not exist anywhere in reflow_report.py, entailment_judge.py or provenance_generator.py today.

**2. Run it on the CURRENT artifact before layering anything on top.** It flags 11 mis-binds shipping right now (#5, #14, #23, #25, #37, #52, #53, #57, #168, #208, #222). #222 ("That same study..." [104] after [95]) is a live false attribution. This is a pre-existing faithfulness breach and it should be fixed on its own merits, independent of L8 — it is arguably the most valuable finding here.

**3. Repair those 11 by DELETION or ANTECEDENT-HOISTING, never by substitution.** Deletion is already licensed by the revised contract ("deletion cannot fabricate") and needs only an audited drop-list entry. Antecedent-hoisting is a pure reorder that the new gate in (1) now validates. **Drop L3's named-referent substitution from L8's dependency entirely** — a byte-substring check keyed to the sentence's own [n] cannot validate a cross-sentence binding, and the key is wrong 79% of the time on this corpus.

**4. Only then build the opening puzzle, from the anaphor-free subset.** Select the two conflicting class-F facts such that (a) neither sentence matches any anaphor lexeme, and (b) hoisting them orphans no successor — i.e. the successor of each vacated slot still passes the gate in (1). Kill the #3 "Within this framework" orphan by hoisting the existing F sentence that NAMES the framework ("a more cautious task-based framework by Acemoglu et al. estimates...") in front of it — pure reorder, zero substitution, zero leak. The class-I tension sentence is fine exactly as the contract specifies (labeled typed move, no digits, no new proper nouns, premise-anchored, contradiction-screened).

**5. Do not promote on the claimed effect size.** +0.002 to +0.005 sits under the +0.0094 resolution floor at k=5. Fold L8's puzzle into a larger arm and measure the arm, or raise k substantially — but do not spend a faithfulness risk on an unmeasurable gain.

Net: the opening-puzzle idea survives in a narrow, reorder-only form. The orphan-repair half must be rebuilt around a referential-integrity gate rather than a string-substitution rule, and that gate is worth building regardless because the artifact is already shipping false study attributions without it.

Per section: identify pairs/triples of already-on-page FACT sentences from distinct sources that tension or converge, then generate candidate CLASS-A sentences/blocks in the cellcog shape (finding A [byte-identical, already present] -> conflicting finding B [already present] -> reconciliation carrying no new facts -> enumerated candidate explanations -> explicit statement of what the evidence cannot resolve). Three deliverables: (1) 15-25 per-section adjudication blocks; (2) the Contradictions section REBUILT from actual adjudicated disagreements between named on-page studies, replacing the placeholder; (3) Limitations rewritten as research-gap delineation in the report's own voice, plus a dedicated Critical Synthesis section (convergence / outstanding tensions / limitations / future research). Every candidate goes through the CLASS-A gate (see contract): deterministic string bans first, entity-in-premise-set check, mechanism-vocabulary-in-span check, then entailment + contradiction_screen fail-closed. Generation noise applies only to these candidate sentences, and the gate's worst case is a no-op (= Arm 1 baseline), so the arm is monotone-safe.
levers.expected_points: +0.015 to +0.035 overall (insight dim +0.04 to +0.08 = +0.013-0.026; compreh balanced-discussion +0.005-0.008; instr-following literature-review-format +0.003-0.008). Honest midpoint +0.025. Clearly above the floor if the mechanism fires at all.
levers.how_it_fails: Two ways. (a) Gate pass-rate collapse: the class-A rules (no new mechanism vocabulary, entities only from the premise set) kill so many candidates that <15 blocks survive — the arm silently becomes Arm 1. (b) Band failure: the blocks survive but read as bolted-on interstitials; the judge's Good->Excellent band demands an argument SEQUENCE, and inserted blocks between extracted walls may not cross it. (b) is the honest bear case and is exactly what the kill rule measures.
levers.cheap_test: BEFORE spending k=5 judge calls: (1) mechanism-fired count — assert >=15 surviving class-A blocks across >=8 sections and >=6 named-study disagreements in the rebuilt Contradictions section (scripted count, zero cost); (2) adversarial audit — 20 fabricated-adjudication probes (invented mechanism, borrowed entity, smuggled quantity, misattributed stance) must ALL be rejected by the gate, plus scripted verification that every surviving class-A sentence's proper nouns and mechanism noun-phrases are byte/stem-present in its own premise set; (3) cleaner-diff gate as in L-STRUCT.

levers.name: Keyed attribution surfacing (CLASS F+A) — rides inside Arm 2, never scored alone
levers.rubric_criterion: Exclusive Citation of 'High-Quality Journal Articles' :: Verifies that all cited sources are academic, peer-reviewed journal articles (instr w=0.15); Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized (compreh w=0.15)
levers.change: RACE strips every [n] marker and the whole bibliography before judging (verified: 345 markers -> 0), so source quality can ONLY be signalled by naming authors/venues in running prose (the brief's own finding). Inject 'Author (Journal, Year)' prefixes on 40-60 FACT sentences — ONLY on sentences that carry their own [n], with every injected token a byte-substring of the bibliography.json/Crossref record keyed by THAT sentence's own [n]. Marker-less sentences are ineligible: no borrowing, no nearest-neighbour antecedent guessing (that was the refuted fabrication path in old L1). Cap <=1 injection per paragraph to avoid serial-summary texture. The rest of the sentence stays byte-identical to its class-F form.
levers.expected_points: +0.004 to +0.010 overall. Below the +0.0094 floor alone — that is WHY it rides inside the L-ADJ arm rather than burning its own k=5.
levers.how_it_fails: Dense name-dropping reads as serial per-paper summary, which P1 explicitly penalizes ('rather than just serially summarizing individual papers'); or venue names in prose don't move the citation-quality criterion because the judge evaluates it from the (stripped) citation apparatus and defaults low regardless.
levers.cheap_test: Scripted audit: every injection keyed to its own sentence's [n], byte-substring verified against that entry, zero injections on marker-less sentences, remainder-of-sentence byte-identity; cleaner-diff confirms the names survive utils/clean_article.py into the judged text.

levers.name: Wave-2 (gated on L-ADJ surviving the kill rule): deletion-only condensation toward ~5.5k words
levers.rubric_criterion: P1: Clarity and Synthesis in Presenting Sourced Information :: ...Checks for appropriate information density and avoidance of redundancy (readability w=0.15); Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized (compreh w=0.15)
levers.change: bodhi wins at 4,361w and dalpha scores 0.5252 at 4,039w; our 7,742w dilutes per-paragraph density. Deletion-only pass (contract-safe by construction: removing whole FACT sentences can never fabricate) that dedupes near-identical findings (same [n] set, >0.8 stem overlap) and trims the weakest-support redundant sentence of each cluster, targeting ~5.0-5.5k words. Run ONLY after L-ADJ has proven the adjudication skeleton, so what remains is dense adjudicated synthesis rather than a shorter mosaic.
levers.expected_points: +0.005 to +0.015, with a real negative tail (-0.010) if breadth criteria (25% of comprehensiveness weight is 'Breadth of ... Dimensions Covered') pay per distinct finding we delete.
levers.how_it_fails: Comprehensiveness breadth drops faster than P1 density rises; the compressive scoring means both moves are small and the net is unresolvable or negative.
levers.cheap_test: Zero-cost coverage audit before scoring: assert every rubric-named facet (job creation, displacement, transformation, skill demands, wage effects, productivity; each named industry) retains >=2 sourced findings post-cut; dedupe log manually spot-checked on 20 clusters.

result.faithfulness_contract: CONTRACT v2 — target is ADJUDICATION, not hedging. Absolute rule, unchanged and non-negotiable: no fabricated fact, number, quote, entity, or attribution ships, ever; every validator failure DELETES the candidate, nothing repairs it; any prose matching no class is deleted (no fourth unclassed category can exist). TOPOLOGY RULE (new, closes the L1 leak): the validation baseline is snapshotted BEFORE any mutating pass runs; every pass is diffed against its own pre-pass bytes; no stage may launder output into the baseline it is validated against (fix reflow_report.py which currently takes src_words AFTER purge_confessions). CLASS F (fact): byte-identical to a provenance-verified source sentence; loss/invention/mutation/duplication caught by a Counter multiset over a chrome-normalized key (CHROME_ANY_RE-stripped — a closed, content-free literal set; no drop-lists, no per-sentence exemptions, ever). CLASS F+A (attributed fact): a CLASS F sentence plus an attribution prefix whose every token is a byte-substring of the bibliography/Crossref record keyed by an [n] PRESENT IN THAT SENTENCE; marker-less sentences are ineligible — no borrowed keys, no antecedent guessing; the remainder must stay byte-identical to its class-F form. CLASS S (structure): headings and table cells assembled from sidecar data or deterministic derivation only. Legal heading forms: (i) noun-phrase facet label — no finite verb, plus the FULL class-content ban battery now wired into validate_heading (RESULT_VERBS_BANNED, NUMBER_WORDS_BANNED, NEGATED_FINDING_BANNED, ATTRIBUTION_BANNED, CERTAINTY_BANNED, no digits/units/cites, <=12 words, no new proper nouns); (ii) 'x.y Facet: gloss' where the gloss is a deterministic main-clause truncation of an ALREADY-APPROVED class-A sentence, inheriting that sentence's full gate — no free LLM text in a heading, ever; HEADING_ATTACKS fixture permanently extended with the 7 accepted attack strings and the entity-in-premises variants so the fixture can never again certify a defense it lacks. CLASS A (adjudication — replaces hedge-based CLASS I): a sentence that compares, reconciles, ranks, or delimits findings ALREADY ON THE PAGE. Redesign principle: adjudication adds ORDERING AND WEIGHT to existing facts, never new facts — so confident evaluative stance about the RELATION between cited findings is legal ('X's estimate rests on a broader occupational sample than Y's'; 'the disagreement narrows once exposure is distinguished from adoption'; 'the evidence cannot yet resolve whether...'), while unearned certainty about the WORLD stays banned (CERTAINTY_BANNED retargeted to 'proves/definitively establishes/guarantees', no longer to stance itself). Rules, all fail-closed: (1) premise set = >=2 class-F sentences from >=2 distinct sources in the same section, physically adjacent; the class-A sentence carries no [n] of its own; (2) no digits, units, or NUMBER_WORDS quantities not byte-present in a premise; (3) proper nouns only if byte-present in the PREMISE SET (not the section, not the bibliography — the premise set), and attribution verbs only with such an entity as subject; (4) mechanism/causal noun-phrases must be stem-present in at least one premise span — a new mechanism is a kill, exactly preserving the entailment judge's NEUTRAL-clause boundary; (5) NLI entailment screen over the concatenated premise spans: entailed, or a comparison whose atoms are each individually entailed; (6) contradiction_screen against all section FACTs. GUARANTEE, restated honestly: a class-A sentence cannot introduce a fact, number, entity, mechanism, or attribution because each is blocked by a deterministic string/stem check keyed to its own premise set, backstopped by fail-closed entailment — and headings can no longer bypass this because they either have no predicate (form i) or inherit a gated sentence (form ii).
result.expected_total: Honest sum from 0.4382: L-STRUCT +0.010/+0.017, L-ADJ +0.015/+0.035, L-ATTR +0.004/+0.010 (rider), hygiene riders ~+0.001 (unmeasurable, claimed as zero). Wave-1 total: +0.030 to +0.055, midpoint ~+0.042 -> ~0.48 (range 0.468-0.493). L-DENSE, if it survives its gate, adds +0.005/+0.015 -> best case ~0.50. NO, this does not reach 0.54 — we are honestly ~0.05-0.06 short. The residual is architectural, not parametric: bodhi's 0.5441 comes from prose that is AUTHORED as adjudicated synthesis at 59w/paragraph density, and no report->report pass can retrofit that texture sentence-by-sentence onto a 7.7k-word extraction mosaic. A later wave must move generation-time: a gated generative composer that authors class-A-dense prose natively at ~4.5-5.5k words, verified per-sentence by entailment against retrieved spans (the machinery already exists in provenance_generator.py/entailment_judge.py; the change is using it to admit authored synthesis rather than only extracted bytes). That wave costs real composes (~65 min each, generation noise ~0.016) and a re-proof of the contract at generation time — it should only be funded if the L-ADJ kill test passes, because it is the same thesis at higher stakes.
result.kill_rule: The core thesis is: 'gated adjudication density is what separates 0.4382 from 0.54 — the judge pays for adjudicative moves in the 64%-weight dimensions.' KILL TEST: Arm 2 (L-ADJ) vs Arm 1 (L-STRUCT), k=5 paired on byte-identical artifacts. PRE-GATE (zero judge calls): if <15 class-A blocks survive the gate, the mechanism never fired — fix the gate/generator before scoring; do not score a no-op. KILL CONDITION: if (Arm2 - Arm1) overall < +0.0094 (the 2-sigma resolvable floor) OR the insight dimension delta is <= 0, the thesis is DEAD as a report->report retrofit: stop building class-A tooling on the banked artifact, do NOT fund L-DENSE, and do NOT fund the wave-2 generative composer on this evidence — the residual explanation shifts to composer-native prose quality requiring a from-scratch architecture decision with fresh evidence. Secondary kills: (a) L-STRUCT: if its k=5 readability delta < +0.0094 despite the cleaner-diff proving the structure survived into the judged text, structure-parity is falsified — do not build any ordering/L8 pass; (b) STANDING JUDGE-VISIBILITY GATE on every future lever: any report->report transform whose output, after utils/clean_article.py, diffs trivially against the banked cleaned baseline is invisible to the grader and MUST NOT be scored (this 60-second check would have killed the refuted L1 before it was written); (c) STANDING FLOOR GATE: any lever whose arithmetic ceiling (criterion weight x maximum raw headroom x 0.037/raw-point) is below +0.0094 may only ship as an unmeasured rider, never as an arm.
result.ceiling_honesty: What this plan CANNOT do, and why. (1) It cannot reach 0.54: overall = ours/(ours+reference) is compressive (+1.0 raw on EVERY criterion = only +0.036), and the Good->Excellent band requirement means retrofitted structure and inserted adjudication blocks top out around 0.48-0.50 — crossing into Excellent on the insight criteria demands an argument SEQUENCE authored through the prose, which insertion between extracted walls cannot fake (the admitted failure mode of mechanical reflow: well-shaped paragraphs with no argument thread). (2) It cannot reach bodhi's density regime: 59w median paragraphs at 4.4k words means ~40% of our words must go; deletion-only condensation that deep risks the comprehensiveness breadth criteria (25% of a 0.29-weight dimension) faster than P1 pays, so L-DENSE is capped and gated. (3) It cannot fix P1's authored-synthesis texture ('synthesizing from multiple sources... rather than serially summarizing') sentence-by-sentence: 96% of our sentences are byte-identical extractions by contract, and that is the moat we are explicitly not selling. (4) It cannot buy anything from the L1-class debris/scrub family: RACE's cleaner already deletes 96% of that surface, and the criterion's entire headroom (+0.0035 at a perfect 10) is 2.6x below the measurement floor — permanently closed as a lever class. (5) The cross-dimension bleed channel is real but unfalsifiable at our noise floor as a standalone claim; we ride it, we never budget for it. The 0.54 destination requires the wave-2 generation-time bet (gated generative composer, ~4.5-5.5k authored words, per-sentence entailment verification), which this plan deliberately defers behind the L-ADJ kill test because it is the same adjudication thesis at 10x the cost, and we should not pay for it before the cheap version has proven the judge actually pays for gated adjudication.
